subject: Geology
title: Contact Metamorphism
description: A micro-lesson that makes Contact Metamorphism usable.

hook.text: Hot magma invades limestone, and a halo of marble forms around it. Heat alone can transform rock on contact.

buildup.text: Contact metamorphism occurs when hot magma intrudes into cooler surrounding rock, baking it with intense heat.

discovery.text: The affected zone, called an aureole, can range from centimeters to kilometers wide depending on magma size.

twist.text: Pressure plays a minor role here. It's mainly heat that drives the mineral changes in contact metamorphism.

climax.text: Contact metamorphism creates hornfels, marble, and quartzite near igneous intrusions. Heat is the key agent.

punchline.text: Magma bakes its neighbors into new rock.
